title: Contract No. 32100070 Amendment: Urban Ore, Inc. for Salvage Operations at the City of Berkeley Transfer Station

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute a contract amendment with
Urban Ore, Inc. for salvage operations of reusable materials at the City's Transfer
Station, increasing the contract by $75,000 from $189,848 to a new not to exceed
amount of $264,848 and extending the term through June 30, 2027.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Funding for this amendment is available in the FY2026 adopted budget in Zero Waste
Fund 601.
sections.current_situation:
For the past 25 years, Urban Ore has salvaged more than 800 tons annually of reusable
materials from the City of Berkeley's Transfer Station. Those items are resold at Urban
Ore’s retail store located at the intersection of Ashby Avenue and 7th Street in West
Berkeley.
This contract extension will allow the City to continue to reduce materials to be
landfilled, supporting increased diversion and the City’s Zero Waste Goal. The
amendment will increase the not-to-exceed amount to cover regular operations and
extend existing service levels until June 30, 2027.
The Urban Ore salvage program is a Strategic Plan Priority Project that advances our
goal to be a global leader in addressing climate change, advancing environmental
justice, and protecting the environment.
sections.background:
Since 2000, the Urban Ore Salvage and Recycling operation has stationed a box truck
and staffing at the City's Transfer Station during regular business hours to salvage
reusable household goods and building materials from discarded objects. Urban Ore
staff identify reusable items from loads delivered to the Transfer Station by Zero Waste
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● Tel: (510) 981-7000 ● TDD: (510) 981-6903 ● Fax: (510) 981-7099
E-Mail: manager@berkeleyca.gov Website: https://berkeleyca.gov/
Page 2 of 3
Contract Amendment No. 32100070: Urban Ore, Inc. for
Salvage Operations at the City of Berkeley Transfer Station CONSENT CALENDAR
January 27, 2026
employees. In many cases, discarded materials are salvaged and prepared for resale,
which allows those materials to be diverted from waste streams.
For more than a decade, Urban Ore has been the only local salvage operation able to
provide a suitable diversion service on-site at the City’s Transfer Station, while
maintaining a local retail outlet for salvaged goods and materials. Urban Ore’s proven
safety record, environmental commitment, and quality customer service have made
them an important City partner.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
The Urban Ore mission statement, “our purpose is to end the age of waste by
advocating and developing total recycling,” demonstrates a committed environmental
vision with recycling as a key component to all aspects of their operation. Urban Ore
has reclaimed more than 800 tons annually from the City’s Transfer Station, which
increases the City's diversion rate, and reduces greenhouse gas emissions.
sections.rationale:
This salvage and reuse/recycling program supports increased diversion of materials
from landfills and helps move the City toward its Zero Waste Goal. In addition, the
Transfer Station’s operational expenses associated with waste hauling are also
reduced.
sections.alternatives:
Opting to discontinue this salvageable waste diversion program will increase City use of
area landfills, increase greenhouse gas emissions from long-haul transportation of
waste materials, and will negatively impact the Zero Waste Division budget. Alternative
service providers to operate this contract have not been identified, due to the unique
nature of this work and the requirements for a nearby storefront to resale the
salvageable items.
CONTACT PERSON
Leticia Jauregui, Solid Waste and Recycling Division Manager (510) 981-6362

1: Resolution
Page 2

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32100070 AMENDMENT: URBAN ORE, INC. FOR SALVAGE
OPERATIONS AT THE CITY'S TRANSFER STATION
WHEREAS, Urban Ore is the only local salvage operation able to provide a suitable
diversion service on-site at the Transfer Station, which reduces landfill-bound waste by
800 tons each year by returning useable materials to the community; and
WHEREAS, this salvage and reuse/recycling program helps move the City toward its zero
waste goals, while reducing the Transfer Station’s operational expenses associated with
increased landfill use, and reducing greenhouse gas emissions caused by long-haul
trucks; and
WHEREAS, Urban Ore’s proven safety record, environmental commitment, and quality
customer service have made them an important zero waste partner; and
WHEREAS, Council on July 1, 2020, by Resolution 69,434-N.S. Council authorized
Contract No. 32100070; and,
WHEREAS, on June 1, 2023, the City Manager of the City of Berkeley authorized an
amendment extending the Contract through June 30, 2025; and,
WHEREAS, on July 25, 2023, by Resolution No. 71,003, the Berkeley City Council
authorized an amendment of said Contract; and,
WHEREAS, funding for this contract amendment is available in Zero Waste Fund 601.
WHEREAS, the Urban Ore salvage program is a Strategic Plan Priority Project that
advances our goal to be a global leader in addressing climate change, advancing
environmental justice, and protecting the environment; and
WHEREAS, opting to discontinue this salvageable waste removal and diversion program
will increase City use of area landfills, and negatively impact the Zero Waste Division
budget.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley authorizes
the City Manager to execute a contract amendment with Urban Ore, Inc. for salvage
operations at the City's Transfer Station increasing the Contract by $75,000 from
$189,848 to $264,848 for a new not to exceed amount of $264,848 and extendi
